source_pr.title: planner: Fix Join reorder occurs "index out of range" error
source_pr.body:
<!-- Thank you for contributing to TiDB!

PR Title Format:
1. pkg [, pkg2, pkg3]: what's changed
2. *: what's changed

-->

### What problem does this PR solve?

Issue Number: close #24095 <!-- REMOVE this line if no issue to close -->

Problem Summary:

```
for i, col := range p.Schema().Columns {
  if !col.Equal(nil, originalSchema.Columns[i]) {
	  schemaChanged = true
	  break
  }
}
```
Panic when `len(p.Schema().Columns) != len(originalSchema.Columns)`

### What is changed and how it works?


### Related changes

### Check List <!--REMOVE the items that are not applicable-->

Tests <!-- At least one of them must be included. -->

- Unit test

### Release note <!-- bugfixes or new feature need a release note -->
- Fix Join reorder occurs "index out of range" error

symptoms: tk.MustExec("desc format = 'brief' select count(*) from t join (select t.id, t.value v1 from t join t t1 on t.id = t1.id order by t.value limit 1) v on v.id = t.id and v.v1 = t.value;") (planner/core/integration_test.go)
failure_signature: tk.MustExec("desc format = 'brief' select count(*) from t join (select t.id, t.value v1 from t join t t1 on t.id = t1.id order by t.value limit 1) v on v.id = t.id and v.v1 = t.value;") (planner/core/integration_test.go)
root_cause_explanation: The test was flaky because it asserted an exact plan/EXPLAIN output that can vary between runs. Exact plan/EXPLAIN assertions are brittle when optimizer choices can vary. This PR stabilizes it by: tk.MustExec("desc format = 'brief' select count(*) from t join (select t.id, t.value v1 from t join t t1 on t.id = t1.id order by t.value limit 1) v on v.id = t.id and v.v1 = t.value;") (planner/core/integration_test.go).
fix_pattern: tk.MustExec("desc format = 'brief' select count(*) from t join (select t.id, t.value v1 from t join t t1 on t.id = t1.id order by t.value limit 1) v on v.id = t.id and v.v1 = t.value;") (planner/core/integration_test.go)

evidence: evidence: tk.MustExec("desc format = 'brief' select count(*) from t join (select t.id, t.value v1 from t join t t1 on t.id = t1.id order by t.value limit 1) v on v.id = t.id and v.v1 = t.value;") (planner/core/integration_test.go)
